segments.text: There's one last plague I will bring down on Pharaoh and on Egypt. After that he will let you go, but when he does, he'll expel every one of you from the country.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Now go and tell the Israelites, both men and women, to ask their Egyptian neighbors for silver and gold objects.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: "The Lord made the Egyptians look favorably on the Israelites. In fact, Moses himself was highly respected in Egypt by both Pharaoh's officials and the ordinary people.
This is what the Lord says: Around midnight I will go through the whole of Egypt.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Every firstborn son in the land of Egypt will die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh sitting on his throne to the firstborn of the servant girl working with a handmill, as well as all the firstborn of the cattle.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: There will be loud cries of mourning all over Egypt, such as have never been before, and will never be again.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: But among all the Israelites there won't even be the sound of a dog barking at them or their animals. That way you will know that the Lord distinguishes between Egypt and Israel.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: All your officials will come to me, bowing down before me and saying, ‘Leave, and take everyone who follows you with you!’ After that I will leave.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Moses was very angry, and left Pharaoh.
Pharaoh is refusing to listen to you so I can do even more miracles in Egypt.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: "Moses and Aaron did these miracles before Pharaoh, but the Lord gave Pharaoh a stubborn attitude, and he wouldn't let the Israelites leave his country.
